title: In Praise Of Dorset
content:
We Doï¿½set, though we mid be hwomely,
Beï¿½nt asheamed to own our pleace;
Anï¿½ weï¿½ve zome women not uncomely;
Nor asheamed to show their feace;
Weï¿½ve a mead or two woï¿½th mowen,
Weï¿½ve a ox or two woï¿½th showen,
In the village,
At the tillage.
Come along anï¿½ you shall vind
That Doï¿½set men donï¿½t sheame their kind.
Friend anï¿½ wife,
Fathers, mothers, sisters, brothers,
Happy, happy, be their life!
Vor Doï¿½set dear,
Then giï¿½e woone cheer;
Dï¿½ye hear? woone cheer!
If you in Doï¿½set be a roamen,
Anï¿½ haï¿½ business on a farm,
Then woont ye zee your eale a foamen!
Or your cider down to warm?
Woont yo have brown bread a-put ye,
Anï¿½ some vinney cheese a-cut ye?
Butter?ï¿½Rolls oï¿½t!
Cream?ï¿½why bowls oï¿½t!
Woont ye have, in short, your vill,
A-giï¿½ed wiï¿½ a right good will? Friend anï¿½ wife,
Fathers, mothers, sisters, brothers,
Happy, happy, be their life!
Vor Doï¿½set dear,
Then giï¿½e woone cheer;
Dï¿½ye hear? woone cheer!

An woonï¿½t yo have vor evï¿½ry shillen,
Shillenï¿½s woï¿½th at any shop.
Though Doï¿½set chaps be up to zellen
Anï¿½ can meake a tidy swop?
Use ï¿½em well, theyï¿½ll use you better;
In good turns they woont be debtor,
Anï¿½ so comely,
Anï¿½ so hwomely,
Be the maidens, if your son
Took woone oï¿½m, then youï¿½d cry ï¿½Well done!ï¿½
Friend anï¿½ wife,
Fathers, mothers, sisters, brothers,
Happy, happy, be their life!
Vor Doï¿½set dear,
Then giï¿½e woone cheer;
Dï¿½ye hear? woone cheer!
meaning:
About: William Barnes (22nd February 1801 - 7th October 1886) was an English writer, poet, minister, and philologist. 
He wrote over 800 poems, some in Dorset dialect and much other work including a comprehensive English grammar quoting from more than 70 different languages.
